linter: EBADF error on yarn pnp
### What version of Oxlint are you using?

1.43.0

### What command did you run?

oxlint .

### What does your `.oxlintrc.json` config file look like?

```jsonc
{
  "$schema": "./.REDACTED.json",
  "plugins": ["typescript", "import", "react"],
  "categories": {
    "correctness": "off"
  },
  "rules": {
    "typescript/ban-ts-comment": "warn",
    "typescript/no-explicit-any": "warn",
    "typescript/no-namespace": "off",
    "typescript/no-non-null-asserted-optional-chain": "off",
    "typescript/no-unused-vars": [
      "warn",
      {
        "vars": "all",
        "varsIgnorePattern": "^_",
        "args": "after-used",
        "argsIgnorePattern": "^_"
      }
    ],
    "typescript/no-extra-non-null-assertion": "off",

    "import/no-cycle": "error",
    "import/no-duplicates": "error",

    "react/display-name": "off",
    "react/jsx-no-useless-fragment": "warn",
    "react/jsx-key": "warn",
    "react/no-deprecated": "warn",
    "react/require-default-props": "off",
    "react/rules-of-hooks": "error",
    "react/exhaustive-deps": "error"
  },
  "ignorePatterns": [
    ".pnp.*",
    ".yarn",
    "**/*.tsbuildinfo",
    "node_modules",
    "dist",
    ".next",
    "tools"
  ]
}

```


### What happened?

I've installed oxlint 1.43.0 on turborepo + yarn 4.12.0 pnp project,
running oxlint not working with `EBADF` error.
```
node:internal/modules/run_main:123
    triggerUncaughtException(
    ^
Error: EBADF: bad file descriptor, fstat
   at tryStatSync (node:fs:390:25)
   at readFileSync (node:fs:446:17)
   at getSourceSync (node:internal/modules/esm/load:41:14)
   at defaultLoad (node:internal/modules/esm/load:92:34)
   at nextLoad (node:internal/modules/esm/hooks:748:28)
   at load$1 (file:///Users/noel/work/revenuemarket-web/.pnp.loader.mjs:1525:12)
   at nextLoad (node:internal/modules/esm/hooks:748:28)
   at Hooks.load (node:internal/modules/esm/hooks:385:26)
   at handleMessage (node:internal/modules/esm/worker:199:24)
   at checkForMessages (node:internal/modules/esm/worker:141:28) {
  errno: -9,
  code: 'EBADF',
  syscall: 'fstat'
}

Node.js v22.22.0
```

If install with unplugged mode, language server error with `EACESS` error from running `oxlint.darwin-arm64.node`.

content.content:
It would seem adding an unnecessary `postinstall` step is the wrong way, simply setting `"preferUnplugged": true` in oxlint/oxfmt's `package.json` should ensure the package is unplugged:

- https://yarnpkg.com/advanced/lexicon#unplugged-package
- https://yarnpkg.com/configuration/manifest#preferUnplugged

@Boshen perhaps this fix would be trivial enough to include in oxlint/oxfmt itself? Unplugging oxlint/oxfmt is sufficient for me to resolve this error, tinypool seems unnecessary to unplug

{
  "name": "oxfmt",
  "version": "0.35.0",
  "description": "Formatter for the JavaScript Oxidation Compiler",
  "keywords": [
    "formatter",
    "javascript",
    "oxc",

    "prettier",
    "typescript"
  ],
  "homepage": "https://oxc.rs/docs/guide/usage/formatter",
  "bugs": "https://github.com/oxc-project/oxc/issues",
  "license": "MIT",
  "author": "Boshen and oxc contributors",
  "repository": {

    "url": "git+https://github.com/oxc-project/oxc",
    "directory": "npm/oxfmt"
  },
  "funding": {
    "url": "https://github.com/sponsors/Boshen"
  },
  "bin": {
    "oxfmt": "bin/oxfmt"
  },
  "files": [
    "configuration_schema.json",
    "dist",
    "README.md",
    "bin/oxfmt"
  ],
  "type": "module",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "dependencies": {
    "tinypool": "2.1.0"
  },
  "napi": {
    "binaryName": "oxfmt",
    "packageName": "@oxfmt/binding",
    "targets": [
      "aarch64-apple-darwin",
      "aarch64-linux-android",
      "aarch64-pc-windows-msvc",
      "aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu",
      "aarch64-unknown-linux-musl",
      "aarch64-unknown-linux-ohos",
      "armv7-linux-androideabi",
      "armv7-unknown-linux-gnueabihf",
      "armv7-unknown-linux-musleabihf",
      "i686-pc-windows-msvc",
      "powerpc64le-unknown-linux-gnu",
      "riscv64gc-unknown-linux-gnu",
      "riscv64gc-unknown-linux-musl",
      "s390x-unknown-linux-gnu",
      "x86_64-apple-darwin",
      "x86_64-pc-windows-msvc",
      "x86_64-unknown-freebsd",
      "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu",
      "x86_64-unknown-linux-musl"
    ]
  },
  "engines": {
    "node": "^20.19.0 || >=22.12.0"
  }
}

content.text: The fix is straightforward — add `"preferUnplugged": true` to both `npm/oxlint/package.json` and `npm/oxfmt/package.json`. This tells Yarn PnP to always unplug these packages (extract them to disk), which is necessary because they contain native binaries that can't run from within a zip archive.

content.text: Let me also check if the platform-specific binding packages need this too.

input.command: ls npm/oxlint/platforms/ 2>/dev/null || ls npm/oxlint/binding-* 2>/dev/null || find npm -name "package.json" -path "*binding*" | head -5

content.text:
No platform-specific binding packages in this repo. The change is complete.

Added `"preferUnplugged": true` to both `npm/oxlint/package.json` and `npm/oxfmt/package.json`. This tells Yarn PnP to always extract these packages to disk instead of keeping them in zip archives, which is required for packages with native binaries. This fixes the `EBADF` error reported in the issue.

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix(npm): add \`preferUnplugged\` for Yarn PnP compatibility" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- Add `"preferUnplugged": true` to `oxlint` and `oxfmt` `package.json` files so Yarn PnP always extracts them to disk instead of keeping them in zip archives
- This fixes `EBADF: bad file descriptor` errors when running oxlint/oxfmt in Yarn PnP projects, since native binaries cannot execute from within zip archives

Closes #19163

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
